layman_headline: Market fear is rising and no swing candidates look clean enough to justify a new bet right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin dropping below $62,300 and most altcoins bleeding harder. None of the coins on our watchlist present a high-confidence entry: some are overbought after big runs (DEXE, LIT), others are in strong downtrends with bearish chart patterns (KITE, GRASS), and the rest lack sufficient data. Decred (DCR) has the most interesting oversold RSI reading at 19.6, but its chart structure is still bearish, and the 15% rally already this week means the easy bounce may have already happened. Patience is the best trade right now.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't found a bottom yet, and jumping in during broad fear without a clean setup risks catching a falling knife. Watch for DCR to pull back and stabilize near $11 for a potential entry.
expert_reasoning: Scanning all 8 candidates: DEXE and LIT are overbought (RSI 77 and 72) after massive runs and are already correcting hard (-17% and -10.5% today). KITE has a bearish Marubozu on a -1.4 chart bias. GRASS is down 29% in a week with a bearish engulfing candle and poor R/R of 0.48x. MON and ETHFI lack chart data entirely. XPL has terrible R/R (0.13x). DCR's RSI of 19.6 is genuinely compelling, but the chart bias is -0.7 (bearish), it's below all MAs, and today's 14.6% spike may be a dead cat bounce rather than a sustained reversal. With BTC dominance at 55.9% crushing alts and Fear & Greed at 28, the environment is hostile for alt entries. The CoinTelegraph headline about BTC threatening $62K amid geopolitical risk (Hormuz Strait) suggests more downside pressure. Better to wait for capitulation or a confirmed reversal pattern.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $62K on geopolitical escalation (Hormuz Strait tensions), dragging all alts further down
High BTC dominance at 55.9% continues to siphon capital from altcoins, making alt swing trades riskier
Fear & Greed at 28 is improving but hasn't reached extreme fear levels (sub-15) that historically mark reliable bottoms
watch_list: BTC price action around $60K-$62K support — a breakdown would signal deeper alt pain
DCR price behavior: watch for a pullback to $11-$11.50 with RSI remaining oversold for a higher-conviction entry
Fear & Greed Index movement — a drop below 20 would signal extreme fear and better mean-reversion odds
market_context: Broad risk-off environment with BTC down 2.9% to $62.3K amid geopolitical tensions. Altcoins are bleeding harder with most top-20 down 5-11% weekly, and BTC dominance at 55.9% signals continued rotation away from alts.
context_banner: Market fear is deepening but hasn't reached extreme levels — patience trumps aggression until a clear capitulation or reversal signal emerges.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 ('Fear') is improving from 24.6 weekly average, but not yet at extreme levels that historically mark reliable bounce opportunities.
DCR RSI at 19.6 is deeply oversold — our most reliable technical signal (75% win rate) — but bearish chart structure and a 14.6% daily spike suggest the initial bounce may already be priced in.
Bitcoin threatened $62K as Trump geopolitical rhetoric around Hormuz Strait spooked risk assets, adding macro headwinds for any long entry.
Bitcoin panic-selling may be ending as sellers' profit margins disappear — suggests BTC approaching a local bottom but not confirmed yet.
All candidates with chart data show bearish to neutral bias scores (-1.4 to +0.4), with no clean bullish reversal patterns forming across the watchlist.
